title: NOC 72402 – Heating, refrigeration and air conditioning mechanics | GTR Immigration
description: In 2026, Heating, Refrigeration and Air Conditioning (HVAC) Mechanics (NOC 72402) are the primary drivers of Canada’s climate-resilient infrastructure. As of January 1, 2026, new federal regulations have mandated a shift to low-GWP (Global Warming Potential) refrigerants, transforming the technical landscape of the trade. Modern HVAC mechanics are no longer just mechanical technicians; they are high-tech environmental specialists managing dual-fuel heat pump systems, AI-driven climate controls, and complex energy-recovery ventilators. Classified as TEER 2, this is a compulsory trade in most provinces, ensuring that only certified professionals handle the high-pressure systems and specialized chemicals essential to Canadian life.

classification.teer: FeatureDetailsBroad Occupational Category7 – Trades, transport and equipment operators and related occupationsTEER Category2 (Requires college, apprenticeship, or significant experience)Major Group72 – Technical trades and transportation officersSub-Major Group724 – Machine repair and maintenance trades

duties: Blueprint Interpretation: Reading and interpreting drawings and specifications to determine layout and installation requirements.
Precision Installation: Assembling and installing refrigeration or air conditioning components (motors, controls, valves, evaporators) using specialized hand and power tools.
Advanced Troubleshooting: Installing, troubleshooting, and overhauling entire integrated HVAC systems using 2026-grade digital diagnostic tools.
Environmental Compliance: Handling refrigerants according to new 2026 HFC phase-down rules, testing for leaks, and recharging systems with low-GWP alternatives.
Maintenance: Calibrating systems, testing regulators, and performing routine preventive maintenance to ensure peak energy efficiency.
employment_requirements: Education: Completion of secondary school is typically required.
Apprenticeship: Completion of a three- to five-year apprenticeship program OR a combination of over five years of work experience and some college courses is required for certification.
Certification: Trade certification is compulsory in Nova Scotia, New Brunswick, Quebec, Ontario, Saskatchewan, Alberta, and British Columbia.
Environmental Handling: A specific Ozone Depletion Prevention (ODP) or equivalent certificate is mandatory for handling refrigerants in all provinces.
Red Seal: The Interprovincial Standards Red Seal is the "Gold Standard," allowing you to work in any province and access high-paying union and industrial roles.
labour_market_demand: The 2026 outlook is Strong, fueled by the national retrofit of aging buildings and the rapid adoption of heat pumps:

Ontario: Very Good prospects. On February 18, 2026, Ontario issued targeted OINP invitations for NOC 72402 under the Employer Job Offer: Foreign Worker stream. The minimum score required was 50, reflecting a high priority for HVAC mechanics to support the province's residential and commercial growth.

British Columbia: High Priority. This NOC is currently on the BC PNP Priority Construction Occupations List. Candidates with a valid trade certificate from SkilledTradesBC benefit from targeted draws and faster PR processing.

Alberta: Strongest Demand. Alberta reports significant hiring difficulties in the HVAC sector. Median wages are among the highest in Canada at $40.00/hr, with top earners in industrial refrigeration reaching $55.00/hr.

National Trend: Over 92% of workers in this NOC are employed full-time. Approximately 34% of the current workforce is expected to retire by 2033, creating a massive demand for international mechanics to fill senior technical and supervisory roles.
Why Work in Canada as an HVAC Mechanic?
Lead the Clean Energy Transition: Gain expertise in 2026-grade technologies, including R-32 and R-454B refrigerant systems and high-efficiency hybrid heating.
Recession-Resistant Career: Your skills are vital for essential infrastructure: hospitals, food warehouses (11% of the sector), and residential high-rises.
Excellent Compensation: As a TEER 2 trade, your median hourly wage in Canada is $37.50/hr, with experienced journeypersons in specialized commercial sectors earning over $105,000 annually.
Priority for PR: HVAC mechanics are a core target for the 2026 Express Entry "Trade Occupations" category and are frequently targeted by OINP and BC PNP draws.
pathways: OINP Employer Job Offer: In February 2026, OINP issued invitations to candidates in NOC 72402 with a score of 50+ under the Foreign Worker stream. This is a prime route for those with an Ontario job offer.
2026 Express Entry (Trade Occupations Category): HVAC mechanics with 12 months of full-time experience (updated 2026 requirement) are eligible for specialized trades draws with lower CRS cutoffs.
BC PNP Construction Stream: Provides priority processing and targeted invitations for HVAC mechanics with a valid trade certificate from SkilledTradesBC.
2026 TR to PR Pathway: A confirmed initiative targeting up to 33,000 temporary residents. HVAC mechanics are classified as "High-Priority Essential Infrastructure Workers."
